meta.description: Disease-specific nutritional intelligence database. Maps diseases to specific protective compounds, mechanisms of action, and evidence-based nutritional strategies. Used by the hybrid pipeline: known diseases use this static DB, unknown diseases trigger the research agent.
meta.sources: PubMed, WHO fact sheets, peer-reviewed nutrition/immunology literature

nutrition_strategy.primary_goal: Immune modulation, reduce viral replication, anti-inflammatory airway support

compounds.name: Quercetin
compounds.mechanism: Inhibits viral neuraminidase activity; reduces inflammatory cytokines IL-6, TNF-α
compounds.evidence: In vitro studies show quercetin inhibits influenza A replication (Choi et al., 2009, J Sci Food Agric)

compounds.name: Allicin
compounds.mechanism: Broad-spectrum antiviral; enhances NK cell activity against infected cells
compounds.evidence: Randomised trial showed garlic supplementation reduced cold/flu incidence by 63% (Josling, 2001, Adv Therapy)

compounds.name: EGCG
compounds.mechanism: Binds HA protein, blocking viral entry into host cells; antioxidant protection for lung tissue
compounds.evidence: EGCG shown to inhibit influenza virus adsorption in MDCK cells (Song et al., 2005, Antiviral Res)

compounds.name: Gingerol
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; reduces airway mucus secretion; mild antiviral activity
compounds.evidence: 6-gingerol inhibits rhinovirus replication in human airway cells (Chang et al., 2013)

compounds.name: Sulforaphane
compounds.mechanism: Activates Nrf2 antioxidant pathway; protects lung epithelium from viral-induced oxidative damage
compounds.evidence: Broccoli sprout extract reduced influenza viral load in smokers (Noah et al., 2014, PLoS One)

additional_nutrients.role: Modulates adaptive immunity; deficiency linked to higher flu susceptibility
Essential for T-cell function; inhibits viral RNA polymerase
Supports neutrophil function; reduces duration of respiratory infections
Precursor to glutathione; thins mucus; reduces oxidative lung damage

dietary_advice: Eat raw crushed garlic daily — wait 10 min after crushing to maximise allicin
Drink 3+ cups green tea/day during flu season for EGCG protection
Add broccoli sprouts to salads — highest natural source of sulforaphane
Ensure adequate vitamin D, especially Oct–Mar in northern latitudes
Hot ginger-lemon-honey tea supports both hydration and anti-inflammatory pathways

mpox.transmission: Close contact, respiratory secretions, contaminated materials

nutrition_strategy.primary_goal: Immune support, skin barrier integrity, anti-inflammatory, antiviral activity

compounds.name: Curcumin
compounds.mechanism: Inhibits NF-κB pathway; reduces viral replication in poxvirus family; anti-inflammatory for skin lesions
compounds.evidence: Curcumin shown to inhibit vaccinia virus (poxvirus) replication (Kim et al., 2020)

compounds.name: Quercetin
compounds.mechanism: Antiviral via interference with viral entry; supports T-cell mediated immunity
compounds.evidence: Quercetin inhibits multiple DNA viruses including poxviruses in cell culture models

compounds.name: Resveratrol
compounds.mechanism: Activates SIRT1 for immune regulation; anti-inflammatory for skin manifestations
compounds.evidence: Resveratrol inhibits vaccinia virus through interference with viral DNA replication (Docherty et al., 2006)

compounds.name: EGCG
compounds.mechanism: Broad-spectrum antiviral; supports interferon response against DNA viruses
compounds.evidence: Green tea catechins enhance innate antiviral immunity (Steinmann et al., 2013)

additional_nutrients.role: Critical for skin barrier integrity and mucosal immune defence
Required for T-cell development; supports skin wound healing
Amino acid that inhibits viral replication in some DNA viruses; supports collagen for skin repair
Lipid-soluble antioxidant; protects skin cells from oxidative damage

dietary_advice: Prioritise vitamin A-rich foods for skin barrier protection (orange/yellow vegetables)
Daily turmeric with black pepper — piperine increases curcumin absorption by 2000%
Zinc-rich foods support both immune function and skin healing
Lysine-rich proteins (fish, eggs) may inhibit poxvirus DNA replication
Maintain hydration and anti-inflammatory diet to support recovery

nutrition_strategy.primary_goal: Block viral entry via ACE2, reduce cytokine storm, protect lung tissue

compounds.name: Quercetin
compounds.mechanism: Computational and in vitro evidence suggests binding to SARS-CoV-2 3CLpro; reduces ACE2 expression
compounds.evidence: Quercetin identified as potential 3CLpro inhibitor (Abian et al., 2020, Int J Biol Macromol)

compounds.name: EGCG
compounds.mechanism: Inhibits SARS-CoV-2 3CLpro; blocks Spike-ACE2 interaction at high concentrations
compounds.evidence: EGCG inhibits SARS-CoV-2 main protease (Jang et al., 2020, Proc Natl Acad Sci)

compounds.name: Curcumin
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; modulates cytokine storm (IL-6, TNF-α); potential Spike protein binding
compounds.evidence: Curcumin reduced ICU admission in COVID patients in randomised trial (Valizadeh et al., 2020)

compounds.name: Sulforaphane
compounds.mechanism: Activates Nrf2 pathway; protects against SARS-CoV-2-induced oxidative stress in lungs
compounds.evidence: Sulforaphane inhibited SARS-CoV-2 replication in vitro and in vivo (Ordonez et al., 2022, Mol Biol Cell)

compounds.name: Allicin
compounds.mechanism: Immunomodulatory; enhances NK cell and macrophage activity
compounds.evidence: Garlic organosulfur compounds show potential anti-SARS-CoV-2 activity (Thuy et al., 2020)

additional_nutrients.role: Modulates inflammatory response; reduces cytokine storm risk; deficiency linked to severe COVID
Inhibits RdRp (RNA polymerase); ionophore combination with quercetin enhances intracellular zinc
SPM (specialised pro-resolving mediators) help resolve lung inflammation
GPx antioxidant enzyme cofactor; deficiency linked to worse COVID outcomes

dietary_advice: Quercetin + zinc combo — quercetin acts as zinc ionophore, driving zinc into cells to inhibit viral replication
Eat fatty fish 2-3x/week for omega-3 SPMs that help resolve lung inflammation
Just 2 Brazil nuts/day provides sufficient selenium
Broccoli sprouts daily for Nrf2-activated lung protection
Anti-inflammatory Mediterranean-style diet pattern reduces severe outcome risk

nutrition_strategy.primary_goal: Immune support, fluid/electrolyte balance, antioxidant protection against haemorrhagic damage

compounds.name: Curcumin
compounds.mechanism: Potent anti-inflammatory; may reduce endothelial damage in haemorrhagic fever
compounds.evidence: Curcumin modulates vascular inflammation pathways relevant to viral haemorrhagic fevers

compounds.name: Resveratrol
compounds.mechanism: Protects endothelial cells; activates SIRT1 for cellular stress resistance
compounds.evidence: Resveratrol shown to protect against vascular permeability in inflammatory models

compounds.name: Quercetin
compounds.mechanism: Stabilises mast cells; reduces vascular permeability; broad antiviral
compounds.evidence: Quercetin's mast cell stabilisation relevant to reducing haemorrhagic complications

compounds.name: EGCG
compounds.mechanism: Antioxidant protection; supports immune cell function under oxidative stress
compounds.evidence: EGCG supports endothelial barrier function (Kim et al., 2014)

additional_nutrients.role: Critical for managing fluid loss in haemorrhagic fever
Supports vascular integrity; antioxidant against oxidative burst
Replenish blood loss — but avoid excess as pathogens exploit free iron
High-quality protein for tissue repair and immune cell production

dietary_advice: Hydration is the MOST critical factor — oral rehydration solutions if available
Anti-inflammatory foods to reduce vascular damage (turmeric, berries, omega-3)
Protein-rich foods for immune system rebuilding
Vitamin C-rich foods to support blood vessel integrity
NOTE: Nutrition is supportive only — Ebola REQUIRES medical treatment

nutrition_strategy.primary_goal: Rehydration, gut barrier restoration, antimicrobial support

compounds.name: Allicin
compounds.mechanism: Antimicrobial against Vibrio species; supports gut microbiome balance
compounds.evidence: Garlic extract showed inhibitory activity against Vibrio cholerae (Eja et al., 2007)

compounds.name: Gingerol
compounds.mechanism: Anti-emetic; reduces gut inflammation; mild antimicrobial
compounds.evidence: Ginger reduces severity of cholera toxin-induced diarrheal response in animal models

compounds.name: EGCG
compounds.mechanism: Supports gut epithelial barrier; antimicrobial activity against gram-negative bacteria
compounds.evidence: Green tea polyphenols show activity against enteric pathogens (Friedman, 2007)

additional_nutrients.role: CRITICAL — cholera causes severe dehydration; WHO-ORS formula is primary treatment
Reduces duration and severity of diarrhoea by 25%; WHO-recommended
Restore gut microbiome after Vibrio displacement
Supports intestinal mucosal repair

dietary_advice: CRITICAL: Oral Rehydration Solution is the primary life-saving intervention
Zinc supplementation reduces diarrhoea duration by ~25% (WHO protocol)
Once tolerating food: rice porridge, bananas, boiled potatoes (BRAT-like diet)
Probiotic foods (yoghurt, kefir) to help restore gut flora after recovery
Ginger tea for anti-nausea support alongside rehydration

nutrition_strategy.primary_goal: Immune recovery, vitamin A repletion, reduce complications

compounds.name: Quercetin
compounds.mechanism: Antiviral and anti-inflammatory; supports immune recovery
compounds.evidence: Flavonoids support immune function during viral infections

compounds.name: Curcumin
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; reduces post-measles immune suppression effects
compounds.evidence: Curcumin modulates immune recovery pathways

additional_nutrients.role: WHO-RECOMMENDED: Vitamin A supplementation reduces measles mortality by 50% in deficient populations
Supports immune reconstitution after measles-induced immunosuppression
Supports immune cell function; helps reduce duration of complications
Essential for immune system rebuild — measles causes immune 'amnesia'

dietary_advice: Vitamin A supplementation is WHO-recommended for all measles cases in children
Orange/yellow fruits and vegetables daily for beta-carotene (provitamin A)
High-protein diet to support immune system recovery (measles causes 'immune amnesia')
Zinc-rich foods to help rebuild depleted immune cells
NOTE: VACCINATION is the primary prevention — nutrition is supportive

nutrition_strategy.primary_goal: Platelet support, prevent haemorrhagic progression, fluid balance

compounds.name: Quercetin
compounds.mechanism: Inhibits NS2B-NS3 protease; may reduce viral replication; supports platelet count
compounds.evidence: Quercetin identified as dengue NS3 protease inhibitor (Senthilvel et al., 2013)

compounds.name: Curcumin
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; reduces vascular permeability in dengue haemorrhagic fever
compounds.evidence: Curcumin modulates dengue-relevant inflammatory pathways (Padilla-S et al., 2014)

compounds.name: EGCG
compounds.mechanism: Inhibits dengue virus entry; antioxidant protection
compounds.evidence: EGCG shown to inhibit dengue virus in cell culture (Carneiro et al., 2016)

additional_nutrients.role: Clinical evidence for increasing platelet count in dengue
food_sources: Fresh papaya leaf juice (traditional remedy with emerging evidence)

additional_nutrients.role: Supports capillary integrity; antioxidant during viraemia
Support blood cell production if platelet/RBC counts drop
Preventing dehydration is critical in dengue management

dietary_advice: Papaya leaf juice has clinical evidence for boosting platelet count — traditional but supported
Stay VERY well hydrated — dehydration worsens dengue complications
Avoid aspirin/NSAIDs (increase bleeding risk) — nutrition support is key
Vitamin C-rich fruits for capillary integrity
NOTE: Monitor platelet count — seek medical care for dengue warning signs

nutrition_strategy.primary_goal: Immune support, neuroprotection (Nipah causes encephalitis), anti-inflammatory

compounds.name: Curcumin
compounds.mechanism: Neuroprotective; crosses blood-brain barrier; anti-inflammatory for encephalitis
compounds.evidence: Curcumin's neuroprotective properties well-documented in multiple neuroinflammatory models

compounds.name: Resveratrol
compounds.mechanism: Neuroprotective; activates SIRT1; reduces neuroinflammation
compounds.evidence: Resveratrol protects against viral encephalitis in animal models (Berardi et al., 2011)

compounds.name: EGCG
compounds.mechanism: Crosses blood-brain barrier; antioxidant neuroprotection; broad antiviral
compounds.evidence: EGCG shown to have neuroprotective effects in multiple viral encephalitis models

additional_nutrients.role: Critical for brain cell membrane integrity; anti-neuroinflammatory
Lipid-soluble antioxidant protecting neural tissue
Support neurological function and repair

dietary_advice: Focus on neuroprotective foods — Nipah causes encephalitis (brain inflammation)
Omega-3 rich fish 3x/week for brain-protective DHA
Turmeric with black pepper for bioavailable curcumin reaching the brain
AVOID raw date palm sap (primary transmission route in Bangladesh)
NOTE: No approved treatment exists — supportive care + nutrition is key

marburg.transmission: Fruit bats, then human-to-human via body fluids

nutrition_strategy.primary_goal: Same as Ebola — vascular protection, rehydration, immune support

compounds.name: Curcumin
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; vascular protective in haemorrhagic conditions
compounds.evidence: Curcumin protects endothelial function under inflammatory stress

compounds.name: Quercetin
compounds.mechanism: Stabilises vascular permeability; broad antiviral properties
compounds.evidence: Quercetin reduces vascular leakage in inflammatory models

compounds.name: Resveratrol
compounds.mechanism: Endothelial protection; SIRT1-mediated cellular stress resistance
compounds.evidence: Resveratrol protects vascular barrier function

additional_nutrients.role: Critical rehydration for haemorrhagic fluid loss
Vascular integrity and collagen support
Supports blood clotting factors (relevant in haemorrhagic disease)

dietary_advice: Rehydration is CRITICAL — similar protocol to Ebola
Vitamin K-rich greens may support clotting factor production
Anti-inflammatory diet to reduce vascular damage
High-protein for immune rebuilding during recovery
NOTE: Marburg has up to 88% fatality — REQUIRES immediate medical care

nutrition_strategy.primary_goal: Gut recovery, rehydration, antimicrobial support, microbiome restoration

compounds.name: Gingerol
compounds.mechanism: Potent anti-emetic; reduces nausea via 5-HT3 receptor antagonism; anti-inflammatory for gut
compounds.evidence: Ginger is evidence-based anti-emetic (Ernst & Pittler, 2000, Br J Anaesth)

compounds.name: Allicin
compounds.mechanism: Antimicrobial; supports gut flora balance during infection
compounds.evidence: Garlic compounds show broad antimicrobial activity in gut

compounds.name: EGCG
compounds.mechanism: Supports gut epithelial barrier repair; antimicrobial
compounds.evidence: EGCG strengthens intestinal tight junctions (Watson et al., 2004)

additional_nutrients.role: Replace fluid loss from vomiting/diarrhoea
Restore gut microbiome displaced by viral infection
Reduces duration of diarrhoeal disease (WHO recommendation)

dietary_advice: First 24h: Clear fluids only — ORS, clear broth, diluted apple juice
Ginger tea for nausea relief (evidence-based anti-emetic)
BRAT diet when ready: Bananas, Rice, Applesauce, Toast
Live yoghurt after 48h to begin microbiome restoration
Avoid dairy (except yoghurt), fatty foods, caffeine for 3-5 days

nutrition_strategy.primary_goal: Block DPP4-mediated viral entry, immune modulation, lung protection

compounds.name: Quercetin
compounds.mechanism: May interfere with coronavirus Spike-receptor binding; anti-inflammatory
compounds.evidence: Flavonoids show anti-coronavirus activity across CoV family

compounds.name: EGCG
compounds.mechanism: Inhibits coronavirus proteases; protects lung epithelium
compounds.evidence: EGCG shows activity against multiple coronaviruses

compounds.name: Curcumin
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; reduces lung fibrosis risk from severe pneumonia
compounds.evidence: Curcumin modulates pulmonary fibrosis pathways (Mohanty et al., 2018)

compounds.name: Sulforaphane
compounds.mechanism: Nrf2-mediated lung protection; reduces oxidative damage
compounds.evidence: Sulforaphane protects respiratory epithelium in infection models

additional_nutrients.role: Immune modulation; reduces severe pneumonia risk
Supports GPx antioxidant system in lungs

dietary_advice: Similar protocol to COVID-19 — both are coronaviruses targeting respiratory system
Quercetin + zinc combination for antiviral synergy
Green tea and broccoli sprouts for lung tissue protection
Mediterranean-style anti-inflammatory diet pattern
NOTE: MERS has ~36% fatality rate — immediate medical care essential
